indicator_text: Legislation increases the transparency and accountability of lobbying activities in a way that includes, but is not limited to, the issues set out in (b)–(i)
scoring_rule: Not specified (IBAC framework is a legislative recommendation set, not a scored index)
source_quote: increases the transparency and accountability of lobbying activities in a way that includes, but is not limited to, the issues set out below (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:412)
notes: Generic preamble item; FOCAL's count of "8 categories" appears to exclude this. Kept here as rec3.a so indicator_id aligns with IBAC's labeling. Discussed in section 4.1.3.

indicator_text: Define 'lobbying activity' in legislation in a way that captures any contact with government representatives (with reference to the Scottish approach) made in relation to government or parliamentary functions, and decision-making at the local government level
section_or_category: Definitions of lobbying activity, lobbyist, and government representative
source_quote: defines the following in legislation: i) 'lobbying activity' in a way that captures any contact with government representatives (with reference to the Scottish approach) that is made in relation to: government or parliamentary functions, and decision-making at the local government level (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:415-419)
notes: Discussed in 4.2.1.1 and 4.2.1.3. IBAC explicitly cites Scotland's Lobbying (Scotland) Act 2016 as the model.

indicator_text: Define 'lobbyist' in legislation in a way that focuses on the activity being undertaken, not persons in the business of lobbying
section_or_category: Definitions of lobbying activity, lobbyist, and government representative
source_quote: 'lobbyist' in a way that focuses on the activity being undertaken, not persons in the business of lobbying (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:420-421)
notes: Activity-based rather than role-based definition; intended to sweep in government affairs directors, in-house lobbyists, occasional lobbyists.

indicator_text: Define 'government representative' in legislation to encompass all public officers who may be subject to lobbying activity, including members of parliament, electorate officers, councillors and council officers
section_or_category: Definitions of lobbying activity, lobbyist, and government representative
source_quote: 'government representative' in a way that encompasses all public officers who may be subject to lobbying activity, including members of parliament, electorate officers, councillors and council officers (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:422-424)
notes: Critical scope expansion: Victorian current code excludes MPs (unless ministers) and electorate officers; this would bring them in.

indicator_text: When an MP initiates a meeting with a minister or their adviser, the MP must disclose to the responsible minister's office whether the MP has a private interest in the matter (with 'private interest' defined to include donations or other benefits provided by a person or entity who asked the MP to make representations)
section_or_category: Disclosure obligations for MP-initiated meetings with ministers
source_quote: ensures members of parliament who initiate meetings with a minister or their adviser: i) disclose to the responsible minister's office: whether the member of parliament has a private interest in the matter about which representations are being made (noting that a private interest in this context should be defined to include donations or other benefits provided by a person or entity who asked the member of parliament to make representations to the minister) (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:425-430)
notes: Atomized from (c)(i) first bullet. Discussed in 4.2.1.2. IBAC explicitly broadens 'private interest' to capture donor-driven advocacy.

indicator_text: When an MP initiates a meeting with a minister or their adviser and has a private interest, the MP must disclose to the responsible minister's office the nature of that interest
section_or_category: Disclosure obligations for MP-initiated meetings with ministers
source_quote: the nature of that interest (if the member of parliament has a private interest) (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:431)

indicator_text: When an MP initiates a meeting with a minister or their adviser, the MP must disclose to the responsible minister's office the names of any persons or entities who have made representations to the MP requesting that they lobby the minister or their adviser (regardless of whether the MP has a private interest)
section_or_category: Disclosure obligations for MP-initiated meetings with ministers
source_quote: the names of any persons or entities who have made representations to the member of parliament requesting that they lobby the minister or their adviser (regardless of whether the member of parliament has a private interest in the matter) (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:432-434)
notes: Atomized from (c)(i) third bullet. Triggered regardless of private interest — this is the core anti-channeling provision.

indicator_text: MPs initiating such meetings must maintain records of those disclosures in a form that is auditable and can be made available to appropriate entities, including the lobbying regulator
section_or_category: Disclosure obligations for MP-initiated meetings with ministers
source_quote: maintain records of those disclosures in a form that is auditable, and can be made available to appropriate entities, including the lobbying regulator (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:435-436)
notes: Pairs with (c)(i) — auditability is the enforcement hook.

indicator_text: Lobbyists must document the name and role of the government representative who has been lobbied, published via an easily accessible and searchable register
section_or_category: Lobbyist documentation of contacts (publicly searchable register)
source_quote: requires that lobbyists document their contacts with government representatives, and that this information is published via an easily accessible and searchable register that includes, but is not limited to: i) the name and role of the government representative who has been lobbied (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:437-440)

indicator_text: Lobbyists must document the subject of the lobbying and its purpose, published via an easily accessible and searchable register
section_or_category: Lobbyist documentation of contacts (publicly searchable register)
source_quote: the subject of the lobbying and its purpose (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:441)

indicator_text: Lobbyists must document the intended outcome of the lobbying communication, published via an easily accessible and searchable register
section_or_category: Lobbyist documentation of contacts (publicly searchable register)
source_quote: the intended outcome of the lobbying communication (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:442)

indicator_text: Mandate monthly publication of extracts or summaries of ministerial diaries and ministerial staff diaries capturing the date of the meeting or event
source_quote: mandates the publication of extracts or summaries of ministerial diaries and ministerial staff diaries on a monthly basis, capturing any form of meeting or event (such as attendance at fundraisers), including, but not limited to details of: i) the date of the meeting or event (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:443-446)
notes: Discussed in 4.2.2.1 and 4.2.3.1. Captures fundraisers explicitly. Victoria currently has no requirement to publish ministerial diaries at all.

indicator_text: Mandate monthly publication of ministerial-diary and ministerial-staff-diary extracts capturing who attended
source_quote: who attended (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:447)

indicator_text: Mandate monthly publication of ministerial-diary and ministerial-staff-diary extracts capturing what interests they represented
source_quote: what interests they represented (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:448)

indicator_text: Mandate monthly publication of ministerial-diary and ministerial-staff-diary extracts capturing the issues that were discussed at the meeting or event
source_quote: the issues that were discussed at the meeting or event (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:449)

indicator_text: Require creation and maintenance of records relating to requests to meet, any associated approvals, and formal meetings between a minister or their staff and any person lobbying them (with reference to Queensland, Scotland and Ireland)
source_quote: the creation and maintenance of records in relation to requests to meet, any associated approvals, and formal meetings between a minister or their staff and any person lobbying the minister or their staff (with reference to the approaches taken in Queensland, Scotland and Ireland) (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:467-470)
notes: Discussed in 4.2.3.1 and 4.2.3.2. Approvals are explicitly required (chief-of-staff approval gate, modeled on Queensland 2022 update).

indicator_text: Require inclusion in ministerial diaries and ministerial staff diaries of details of contacts with people undertaking lobbying activity, to support the monitoring and compliance activities of the lobbying regulator (with reference to NSW and Queensland)
source_quote: the inclusion in ministerial diaries and ministerial staff diaries of details of contacts with people undertaking lobbying activity to support the monitoring and compliance activities of the lobbying regulator (with reference to the NSW and Queensland approaches) (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:471-473)

indicator_text: Require publication of a uniform Ministerial Staff Code of Conduct under legislation that obliges ministerial staff to comply with lobbying regulations
source_quote: the publication of a uniform Ministerial Staff Code of Conduct required under legislation, which obliges ministerial staff to comply with lobbying regulations (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:474-475)

indicator_text: Require training for ministerial advisers to raise awareness of risks associated with lobbyists
source_quote: training for ministerial advisers to raise awareness of risks associated with lobbyists (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:476)

indicator_text: Require electorate officers to maintain records (with prescribed details) of contact with those undertaking lobbying activity; records must be auditable to support the monitoring and compliance activities of the lobbying regulator
source_quote: electorate officers to maintain records (outlining prescribed details) of contact with those undertaking lobbying activity, noting that those records must be auditable to support the monitoring and compliance activities of the lobbying regulator (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:479-481)
notes: Discussed in 4.2.3.3. IBAC notes no other Australian jurisdiction extends lobbying regulation to electorate officers — this is a Victoria-specific innovation.

indicator_text: Require the Electorate Officers Code of Conduct to specify that electorate officers must comply with lobbying regulations
source_quote: the Electorate Officers Code of Conduct to specify that electorate officers must comply with lobbying regulations (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:482-483)

indicator_text: Require training for electorate officers to raise awareness of risks associated with lobbyists
source_quote: training for electorate officers to raise awareness of risks associated with lobbyists (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:484)

indicator_text: Prohibit success fees being given or promised to lobbyists in return for a certain outcome of any lobbying activity
source_quote: prohibits success fees being given or promised to lobbyists in return for a certain outcome of any lobbying activity (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:486-487)
notes: Discussed in 4.2.4.1. Broader than Victoria's current procurement-only prohibition; modeled on NSW and Queensland.

indicator_text: Ensure that a lobbyist cannot lobby an elected official whose election they have supported directly or indirectly, for example through donations or in-kind support to a campaign
section_or_category: Cooling-off / separation between political support and lobbying
source_quote: ensures that a lobbyist cannot lobby an elected official whose election they have supported directly or indirectly, for example, through donations or in-kind support to a campaign (papers/text/IBAC_2022__corruption_risks_donations_lobbying.txt:489-490)
notes: Discussed in 4.2.4.2. Modeled on Canadian Lobbyists' Code of Conduct. Distinct from the post-employment cooling-off period (which is in Recommendation 4(c), not Recommendation 3, and is therefore out of scope here).
